steps.title: Two Threats, One Move
steps.text: A double attack is any move that creates two simultaneous threats. The fork is one kind — the same piece attacks two targets. But a move can also create two DIFFERENT kinds of threat: one mate threat plus a material threat, for example.

steps.prompt: The queen is undefended — grab her.
steps.hint: The knight leaps to d5 with a capture.

steps.title: The Principle
steps.text: Every good tactical move threatens something specific. Two threats from one move is often decisive because the opponent can only respond to one of them. Train your eye to count threats — if you see two, something good is happening.
